seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay.
mutation: composite: replace the cardinality budget (remove <=k edges) with a WEIGHTED closure-cost budget (each transect has an individual closure cost, total closed cost <= C), turning the single s-t shortest-path interdiction into a knapsack-flavored selection; network is a large grid-plus-random-chord multigraph with a single cheap current trunk and otherwise expensive links.
title: Choked Current: Coral Reef Survey Interdiction
strategies: do-nothing baseline: close no transects (original fastest AUV route length)
cheapest-first greedy: repeatedly close the lowest-closure-cost edge on the current shortest path that keeps the survey site reachable, until the budget cannot afford any path edge

budget-aware best-improvement local search: each round evaluate the true delay gain of closing every affordable shortest-path edge via a fresh Dijkstra and commit the edge with the best gain-per-cost, keeping s-t connected
randomized multi-restart: perturb the closure order/choices and keep the transect set maximizing the final AUV route length within budget
